title: Boat Rental in Saranda 2026: Best Charter Options and Costs
html:
Chartering a boat in Saranda, the Albanian Riviera's gateway, offers access to the Ionian Sea from the port near the city centre. Private motorboat rentals start around EUR 250 for a half-day excursion. To compare verified listings, hire a boat through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Saranda or list your own.

Where to set off in Saranda

Most charters depart from the Saranda Port, a modern marina with cafés and provisioning shops. Nearby, the smaller Ksamil Islands, just 12 nautical miles south, offer turquoise bays and secluded beaches. A trip to the ancient site of Butrint, accessible by boat from the Ksamil channel, is a popular half-day route. Further north, the Bay of Porto Palermo features a historic castle and clear waters ideal for swimming.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Saranda?

Prices range from EUR 50 for a shared tour to EUR 1,500 for a crewed yacht per day. The exact cost depends on the vessel size, duration, and season.

  • Shared speedboat tour (2-3 hours): EUR 50, 80 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 4 hours): EUR 250, 400
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, up to 12 guests): EUR 800, 1,200
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (up to 8 guests): EUR 1,200, 1,500

BoatHire24 lists vessels with transparent pricing; for semi-private options, the Mefasa 90 is a top choice.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4.5, 6 m): Best for couples or small groups exploring Ksamil and the nearby islets; holds 4, 5 guests; from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (10, 14 m): Ideal for families wanting stability and sun-deck space; sleeps up to 12; from EUR 800 full-day.
  3. Crewed motor yacht (17, 27 m): Suited for luxury day trips to Corfu or the Blue Eye Spring; capacity up to 8; from EUR 1,200 full-day. For an example of this motor yacht, see the Mefasa 90 listing on BoatHire24.
  4. RIB / console boat (6, 8 m): Great for watersports enthusiasts heading to the Blue Eye Spring; holds 6, 8 guests; from EUR 350 half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Saranda?

Yes, for bareboat motorboats over 15 horsepower you need a recognised boating licence; otherwise a skipper is mandatory. RIBs and small outboards often require no licence if under 15 hp. All operators on BoatHire24 confirm the exact requirements before booking.

Local tip: The Ionian Sea can face a sudden ‚bura‘ wind in spring and autumn; always check the forecast and ensure your skipper is experienced with local conditions.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 50, 1,500 per day
  • Peak months: July, August, early September
  • Common trip duration: 4, 8 hours
  • Max guest capacity (typical): 12 on catamarans
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Saranda

Can I rent a boat without a captain in Saranda?

Yes, for small boats up to 15 hp you do not need a licence and may operate them without a skipper, but you must be familiar with the waters.

How long is a typical boat trip from Saranda to Corfu?

A one-way crossing from Saranda to Corfu town takes about 35 minutes by speedboat or 1 hour by standard motorboat.

What is the best month for calm seas in Saranda?

June and September offer settled weather with fewer tourists; the meltemi wind rarely reaches the Albanian coast.

Are there sunset cruises available in Saranda?

Yes, many operators offer 2-hour sunset trips along the Riviera for around EUR 60 per person, often with drinks included.

Exploring Saranda's coastline by boat reveals hidden coves and historic sites. Whether you are a first-time renter or seasoned sailor, BoatHire24 connects you with reliable operators. If you own a vessel, you can list a boat for free and reach thousands of travellers looking to charter in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Saranda and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
